title: psacadocalymma comatum
intro: Psacadocalymma comatum, also known as the "Balsam Apple Tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and colds, and treating respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Psacadocalymma comatum typically involve infusions of the plant's leaves and bark, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications.
